changes.value:
**File:** `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/editors/date-editor.tsx` lines 48-52
**Severity:** warning

The `useEffect` that parses the draft and calls `onChange?.(parsed)` runs on mount with the initial value. If the field already has a valid date, this immediately triggers a debounced save of the existing value -- a no-op write to the backend on every editor open. Other editors (number, color, select) only call `onChange` in response to user interaction, which is the correct pattern.

Fix: Guard with a `hasMounted` ref or skip when `parsed === initial`.
date-editor: onChange in useEffect may fire on mount with the initial value
